title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) enable inference-time compute for coding agents to achieve higher performance and reliability. The approach maps RLM principles onto agentic systems, including implementations like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse, to guide agents through recursively solving complex tasks with declarable outcomes.
writeup: Raymond Weitekamp presents Recursive Language Models (RLMs), an approach that applies inference-time compute to coding agents to raise performance and reliability. The talk maps RLM principles onto agentic systems, walking through implementations including Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse. The core idea: agents recursively decompose and solve complex tasks with declarable outcomes.
